Satsang with Mooji
Satsang with Mooji
So this material world which we all are right now the part of, this material nature,
we all are under the influence of the modes of material nature
which are influencing our senses, our mind, our intelligence,
our ego.
The living entity, the soul, is actually sanatana
and untouched by these material qualities in its true pure form.
Being amsa of the Lord, it is also sat, chit and ananda.
It is eternal.
Full of form.
Full of form.
Full of form.
Full of knowledge and blissful.
But when the soul comes in contact with the material nature
and gets a material body, which we all have now,
under the influence of three modes of material nature,
it loses its original quality of sat, chit and ananda.
This material body which is comprised of the senses, the mind, the intelligence, the ego,
they all get influenced by the material nature or material qualities or the modes of material nature
under the influence of inhabitable time.
The inhabitable time of the three modes of material nature known as prabhda,
so this world is created, this material existence is created by the Lord for the living entities
who want to enjoy independent of him, and the living entity is given a material body
with the senses, with the mind to enjoy.
But this material nature as part of the material existence, the soul is not independent.
It is completely so to say under the influence of the modes.
So here in this verse it says Lord Brahma, now he has, he is the secondary creator.
Aham sarvasya prabhavo matta sarvam pravartate.
Krishna says everything is emanating from me.
But this material world, we also come to understand that how Brahma,
Brahma creates.
So he is the secondary creator.
And there is a time when the purpose for which this creation has been created,
everything which is created has a purpose.
So here it says when the purpose is achieved, even Brahma has a time span, a life span,
and he desires to dissolve it and enter into the kingdom of God, others then enter with him.
So this material world which is created by the Lord is not sanatana,
Prabhupada mentions in the purport, unlike the spiritual world which is sanatana,
which is comprised of spiritual nature, which is part of his internal energy.
Whereas this material world comes into being, stays for a while, and then goes away.
So now we should all understand what are we up to.
We are part of this wonderful mission of Srila Prabhupada, Lord's mission.
What are we trying to achieve? What are we trying to do?
What is the purpose of our life?
We are doing devotional service.
Why are we doing?
Sometimes we can lose the context or lose the bearings.
And at times we wonder, what am I doing?
So we should understand that we are in this material world,
the spirit soul which is eternal has got encased in a material body.
And life after life, because we have so many different kinds of desires,
to fulfill those desires, the living entity engages in different kinds of activities
and gets different kinds of material bodies.
Punarapi janma, punarapi marana.
To fulfill the unfulfilled desires, Krishna who is accompanying the soul,
gives him different kinds of material bodies.
So when we come to this human form of life and
brahmanda brahmite kono bhagyavan jiv,
when we get the mercy of a bona fide spiritual master,
we get to understand who we are.
What is the purpose of this creation?
We understand,
what is the goal of human form of life?
As I mentioned, everything has a purpose.
So the goal is,
to extricate ourselves from the influence of the modes,
to extricate ourselves from this material nature,
to extricate ourselves from this cycle of birth and death,
and go back to our original home, back to Godhead.
That's what Brahma is doing.
That's the goal, that's the sadhya, that is the ultimate purpose.
And that purpose can be achieved in human form of life.
And to achieve that purpose, we have sadhana.
Sadhya is the goal and sadhana is the means or the process,
by which we can achieve the goal.
So we'll go one by one,
as to what great Acharyas, Prabhupada has given us,
as part of our sadhana.
